propositions.text: the Meridian offer came with a 48-hour expiry and my stomach dropped
kept flip-flopping all night about the offer; wrote two resignation drafts and binned both
phoned my old mentor Priya at midnight; she asked one question about mornings
accepted the Meridian offer an hour before the expiry and felt instantly calm
picked the quartz counters for the kitchen after months of browsing samples
the kitchen contractor starts the first week of May
the library extended weekend opening hours
the dentist confirmed the checkup for the ninth
watered the ficus and rotated it toward the window

questions.q: the landlord gave us until Friday to sign the lease renewal and I keep going back and forth — have I been in a situation like this before?
shared_structure: hard time limit forcing a choice

propositions.text: realized I'd driven Dana to the airport four times this year and she's never once offered petrol money
the resentment about Dana crept up so slowly I didn't notice until I dreaded her name on my phone
finally told Dana plainly what I could and couldn't do; shaky voice, held firm
Dana and I are lighter with each other since; the friendship survived the honesty
long run Sundays are sacred now; eighteen kilometres this morning
carb-loading strategy sorted for race week
the corner bakery changed hands again
the parcel locker code changed again
picked up stamps and posted grandma's birthday card

questions.q: my coworker keeps offloading his tickets onto my queue and I'm rehearsing how to raise it without blowing up — does this remind me of anything?

propositions.text: practiced the nocturne four hours a day for the recital; my teacher says it's over-polished
froze eight bars into the recital piece; the room went white
restarted the nocturne at half tempo, dropped the ornaments, and the music came back
my teacher's note afterwards: the simple version moved people; the polish was for me
the accountant wants every receipt from the studio conversion
filed the extension paperwork for the studio's depreciation claim
new neighbours moved into the blue house
the stairwell lightbulb needed replacing
printed the insurance forms for the glovebox

questions.q: I over-rehearsed the investor demo, blanked on slide two, and I'm dreading the next pitch — have I been somewhere like this?

propositions.text: year two of the camper conversion and the electrics still aren't in; I dread the garage
admitted the camper dream belonged to a version of me from 2022
sold the camper shell and the solar kit to a couple who cried with excitement
the empty garage feels like a held breath finally let out
joined the Tuesday choir; we're butchering Brahms beautifully
choir concert booked for December at St Anne's
council resurfaced our street at last
the supermarket moved the oats aisle again
charged the spare batteries for the smoke alarm

questions.q: four drafts into the novel and I'm wondering if shelving the manuscript would be failure or freedom — anything in my past like this?
shared_structure: sunk cost in a long project

propositions.text: three basil pots on the fire escape — my entire agricultural empire
the basil thrived so the fire escape now hosts eight pots and a chilli experiment
signed for the community plot; the fire-escape trial earned it
first full bed planted at the plot; scaling what already worked, nothing speculative
jury duty summons for the last week of September
the trial wrapped early; civic duty complete
my cousin's boat finally sold
booked the annual boiler service
returned the borrowed ladder to Sam

questions.q: instead of committing to the shop lease I could trial a Saturday market stall for a month first — have I approached something this way before?
shared_structure: scaling only after the trial works

propositions.text: the photography account crossed ten thousand followers and I felt nothing
caught myself shooting for the algorithm — golden hour, faces, whatever performs
quit checking the follower graph; started selling prints of the harbour series instead
a stranger emailed that the harbour print hangs over her desk; that one email beat the whole graph
the visa paperwork for mum's visit needs three more documents
mum's visa approved; she lands in October
the gym replaced all the treadmills
the pharmacy switched to earlier closing hours
descaled the kettle at last

questions.q: I keep chasing personal records at the gym and enjoying training less every week — where have I seen this pattern in my own life?

propositions.text: wrist strain from guitar practice; the physio says six weeks of restraint
cut guitar sessions to fifteen careful minutes with a timer; hated every restriction
the shortened guitar practice made me precise; the strain healed ahead of schedule
bought new running shoes for marathon training, the neon ones, plus a running vest
the running club discount got me a knee sleeve and a second pair of running shoes for training volume
the office espresso machine died again
swapped the winter duvet in
the bins schedule shifted for the public holiday

questions.q: my marathon training hit an injury — the knee flared up mid-run and I have to pull my running volume way back — when have I handled something like this?

propositions.text: best-man speech drafted to the minute: eleven pages, colour-coded cue cards
booked flights to Lisbon for my sister's wedding and planned the whole trip itinerary; aisle seats both ways
the wedding hotel is walkable from the venue; trip logistics fully planned and sorted
binned the eleven pages; kept one card with three beats and a joke
renewed my passport two years early
the speech landed — laughter in the right places precisely because I could breathe and improvise
topped up the transit card online
wiped down the balcony furniture for spring

questions.q: I've over-planned this Berlin trip to a fifteen-tab itinerary and it's stressing me out — I want to scrap it for a loose plan — have I learned this lesson somewhere before?

propositions.text: the new intern missed both deadlines his first fortnight; the team wanted him reassigned
set up the same fifteen-minute check-in with the intern every morning, no judgement, same questions
the intern shipped the reporting feature solo; the morning rhythm did what pressure couldn't
dog-sitting Rufus this weekend; the dog's routine demands three walks a day
returned Rufus the dog to the neighbours with a report on his walk routine and park behaviour
the balcony door finally got fixed
the wifi router got its firmware update
bought a new umbrella after the old one inverted

questions.q: the rescue dog we adopted has behaviour problems and everyone says give him up, but I think a steady dog routine could turn him around — when have I bet on patience like this?
shared_structure: rough start others gave up on

propositions.text: the phone carrier billed the cancelled add-on a third time; started a dated log of every call
escalated to the carrier's complaints team with the full log attached; suddenly everyone was helpful
ported my number out anyway — the refund didn't buy back the trust
opened the high-interest savings account for the house deposit at the new bank branch — no monthly account fee
automated the monthly transfer into the bank savings account; deposit fund growing
recycling day moved to Thursdays
labelled the freezer containers properly
the car park barcode scanner works again

questions.q: the bank keeps charging that account fee they promised to waive and support loops me endlessly — I'm ready to move banks — have I run this playbook before?

propositions.text: the band split the wedding-gig fee unevenly and nobody would say so out loud
our drummer quit the band over the money silence, not the money
post-mortem with the band: we agreed the unspoken split killed it, not the amounts
the group holiday kitty caused sulking until Ines built the shared spreadsheet
spreadsheet transparency fixed the holiday kitty mood overnight
wheel-throwing class booked for spring; solo hobby, my clay, my mess
the ferry timetable changed for winter
renewed the library card for two more years
vacuumed behind the sofa, found two pens

questions.q: my friend and I started a little app side-business and the revenue conversation is getting awkward and avoided — what past experience maps onto this?

propositions.text: back from the burnout leave with hard rules: no email past six, one project at a time
caught myself sneaking a second project three weeks in; the old pattern knocking
held the one-project rule for the full quarter; energy actually compounding now
the trainer's plan for Milo is painfully gradual: one calm street corner at a time
skipped ahead to the busy market with Milo once and paid for it with a week of regression
the ridge trail hike was all fog and no view; glorious anyway
the streetlight outside finally got repaired
the office badge photo finally got retaken
recycled the box mountain in the hallway

questions.q: post-surgery rehab says weeks of tiny exercises and I'm itching to rush the recovery — where have I met this temptation before?

propositions.text: spent $600 on the bathroom tiles
spent $450 on the bathroom vanity
paid the plumber $800 for the bathroom rough-in
the ficus needs repotting

questions.q: how much have I spent on the bathroom in total?

propositions.text: the tiler quoted $1,200 for the bathroom walls and floor
the tiler can start the first week of June
borrowed the wet saw from Theo

questions.q: what did I say about the tiler's quote?

propositions.text: the renovation budget felt limitless in January; famous last words
halfway through and the renovation budget spreadsheet has a red tab now
we finish the renovation under a revised budget I actually believe
market on Saturday had the good peaches

questions.q: how has my thinking about the renovation budget evolved?

propositions.text: first renovation quote came in and I laughed, then cried
third renovation quote finally within reach; shortlisted two builders
the car passed inspection first try

questions.q: when did I first get quotes for the renovation?
